items.label: Project jurisdiction
items.hint: GB=BS 7671:2018+A2:2022; KE=KS 1700:2018 routing to BS 7671 via §313; INT=IEC 60364; US=NEC 2023.

items.label: Schematic type
items.hint: Choose control_* for control/starter schemes; protection_* for relay-based protection schemes.

items.label: Which upstream intents are available for this schematic?
items.hint: Leaf-mode fallback applies if all absent. Select all that apply.

items.label: Drawing designation or tag
items.hint: Stable reference (e.g. 'SCH-MCC-01', 'PRO-TR1-87T'). Used in cross-drawing intent envelope.

items.label: Motor starter topology
items.hint: DOL=direct-on-line; star_delta=star-delta reduced voltage; soft_start=electronic soft starter; VFD=variable frequency drive.

items.label: Motor rated output power (kW)
items.hint: From motor nameplate. Used to select contactor, overload relay, and cable sizing.

items.label: Motor rated voltage (V)
items.hint: Line-to-line nameplate voltage (e.g. 415, 400, 480). Must match system voltage.

items.label: Motor full-load current FLA (A)
items.hint: Nameplate full-load amperes at rated voltage. Basis for contactor and overload relay sizing.

items.label: Motor starting inrush current (A)
items.hint: Locked-rotor or peak starting current. Typically 6–8× FLA for squirrel-cage motors.

items.label: Motor start time (s)
items.hint: Time from energisation to rated speed at full load. Drives overload class selection (Class 10 ≤10s, Class 20 ≤20s, Class 30 ≤30s).

items.label: Upstream circuit breaker or fuse rating (A)
items.hint: Overcurrent protective device immediately upstream of the starter. Used in selectivity check.

items.label: ATS changeover topology
items.hint: ATS_3P=3-pole automatic transfer switch; ATS_4P=4-pole (switched neutral); manual=manually operated changeover.

items.label: Primary supply source rating (kVA)
items.hint: Mains or utility supply rating at the ATS input. Used for transfer capacity check.

items.label: Backup supply source rating (kVA)
items.hint: Generator or UPS rating at the ATS alternate input.

items.label: Maximum permissible transfer time (s)
items.hint: Time from mains loss to backup supply availability. Typical: 10–15 s for generator ATS; <20 ms for static transfer switch.

items.label: Protected transformer rating (kVA)
items.hint: From transformer nameplate. Used to derive rated currents and CT primary selection.

items.label: Transformer HV winding voltage (kV)
items.hint: Line-to-line voltage at the high-voltage terminals (e.g. 11.0, 33.0, 6.6).

items.label: Transformer LV winding voltage (kV)
items.hint: Line-to-line voltage at the low-voltage terminals (e.g. 0.415, 0.400, 0.480).

items.label: CT primary current (A)
items.hint: Rated primary current of the measuring CT(s). Select to IEC 61869-2 preferred ratios.

items.label: CT secondary current (A)
items.hint: 1 A secondary for long cabling runs; 5 A secondary for local panel installations.

items.label: CT accuracy class
items.hint: 5P or 10P for overcurrent protection; PX or PS for differential and high-impedance schemes per IEC 61869-2.

items.label: Transformer vector group
items.hint: e.g. Dyn11, YNyn0, Yd1. Required for 87T vector group compensation in differential relay.

items.label: Differential relay bias slope (%)
items.hint: Percentage bias for restrained differential relay (87T or 87B). Typical: 20–40% for transformers, 30–50% for busbars. Omit for high-impedance schemes.

items.label: Motor rated power (hp)
items.hint: US / nameplate horsepower. Used in NEMA MG1 coordination checks for protection_motor schematics.

items.label: Motor service factor (SF)
items.hint: From nameplate. Typical: 1.0–1.15 for NEMA motors. Extends permissible overload relay pickup to FLA × SF.

items.label: Protected busbar voltage (kV)
items.hint: Line-to-line busbar voltage (e.g. 11.0, 33.0, 132.0).

items.label: Number of busbar protection zones
items.hint: Single busbar=1; double busbar or bus-section=2; double busbar with bus-coupler=3.

items.label: Relay family or manufacturer model
items.hint: e.g. ABB REF615, Siemens 7SJ66, Schneider Sepam 80, SEL-710, SEL-387. Used to validate settings against manufacturer typicals.

items.label: Number of sequence steps
items.hint: For control_sequence schematics: total number of timed or interlocked steps in the sequence.
